statement.p: Kirima offered to pay the bill and tip at a restaurant where she and her friends had dinner. In total she paid \$150 even, where the tip was a little more than 19\%\text{.} Wed like to know what was the bill before tip. Set up an equation for this situation.

solution.p: A common mistake is to translate a question like this into what is 19\% of \$150\text{?} as a way to calculate the tip amount, and then subtract that from \$150\text{.} But that is not how tipping works. The 19\% is applied to the original bill, not the final total. So please read carefully.
If we let x represent the original bill, we can apply (1.1.2) and we have:
Again, we are not currently trying to solve this equation, just write it down.
